rationale: The cited paper appears directly relevant to the citing paper’s core problem: both study charitable donations through norm/social-influence cues, and the citing note explicitly frames prior conflict as "positive deviations via social proof" versus "negative deviations via need." The cited note provides a concrete social-proof mechanism in donations (contingent match incentives, plausibility, and conversion effects), so this is plausibly a substantive prior-work/theory source rather than an incidental mention.
